verbs.meaning: To move your legs very fast
verbs.example: I run to the playground every day after school.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: When you go fast with your feet, you run.

verbs.meaning: To push your body off the ground
verbs.example: Can you jump over this puddle? I know you can!
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Jump is when your feet leave the ground.

verbs.meaning: To put food in your mouth and swallow
verbs.example: I eat breakfast at 7 o'clock every morning.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: When you put food in your mouth, you eat.

verbs.meaning: To take liquid into your mouth and swallow
verbs.example: You should drink water when you are thirsty.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Drink is when you take a sip of water, milk, or juice.

verbs.meaning: To rest with your eyes closed
verbs.example: I sleep for 10 hours every night because I need energy.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Sleep is when your body rests at night.

verbs.meaning: To stop sleeping
verbs.example: I wake up when the sun comes through my window.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Wake up is when you open your eyes in the morning.

verbs.meaning: To have fun doing an activity
verbs.example: Let's play hide and seek! I'll count to twenty.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Play is when you have fun with toys or games.

verbs.meaning: To look at and understand written words
verbs.example: I read a story about a dragon every night before bed.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Read is when you look at words in a book and say them.

verbs.meaning: To put words on paper using a pencil or pen
verbs.example: I write letters to my grandma because she lives far away.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Write is when you use a pencil to make words.

verbs.meaning: To make a picture using pencils, crayons, or markers
verbs.example: I draw rainbow unicorns because they are beautiful.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Draw is when you make pictures with crayons.

verbs.meaning: To make music with your voice
verbs.example: I sing my favorite song in the shower every morning.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Sing is when you use your voice to make music.

verbs.meaning: To move your body to music
verbs.example: We dance at parties when happy music plays.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Dance is when you move your body to music.

verbs.meaning: To make a happy sound with your mouth
verbs.example: I laugh when my dad tells silly jokes.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Laugh is the sound you make when something is funny.

verbs.meaning: To have tears come from your eyes because you are sad
verbs.example: I cry when I miss my friend who moved away.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Cry is when tears fall from your eyes.

verbs.meaning: To turn up the corners of your mouth when happy
verbs.example: I smile when I see my puppy running to me.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Smile is when your mouth makes a happy shape.

verbs.meaning: To do something nice for someone
verbs.example: I help my little brother tie his shoes.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Help is when you do something nice for another person.

verbs.meaning: To make something not dirty
verbs.example: I clean my room every Saturday so it looks nice.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Clean is when you wash or pick up things to make them neat.

verbs.meaning: To put things together to make something
verbs.example: I build tall towers with my colorful blocks.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Build is when you put pieces together to make something new.

verbs.meaning: To send something through the air using your hand
verbs.example: I throw the ball to my friend during catch.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Throw is when you send a ball through the air.

verbs.meaning: To grab something that is moving through the air
verbs.example: I catch the ball with both hands so I don't drop it.
verbs.kid_friendly_explanation: Catch is when you grab something before it hits the ground.
